message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Introduction</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Variants of Concern of SARS-CoV-2 (VOCs), the new coronavirus responsible for COVID-19, have emerged in several countries. Two mutations in the gene coding for the Spike protein of the viral genome are particularly important and associated with some of these variants: E484K and N501Y. Restriction enzyme analysis is proposed as a rapid method to detect these two mutations.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methodology</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A search on GISAID was performed in April 2021 to detect the frequency of these two mutations in the sequence available and their association with other lineages. A small amplicon from the Spike gene was digested with two enzymes: HpyAV, which allows detecting E484K mutation, and MseI, for detecting the N501Y one.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The mutations E484K and N501Y, associated with VOCs, have emerged in several other lineages, particularly E484K. A 100% correlation was observed with sequencing results.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The proposed methodology, which allows screening a great number of samples, will probably help to provide more information on the prevalence and epidemiology of these mutations worldwide, to select the candidates for whole-genome sequencing.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Importance of E484K and N501Y mutations in SARS-CoV-2 for genomic surveillance: rapid detection by restriction enzyme analysis

reference.article-title: Biología y evolución del coronavirus causante de la COVID-19
Estimated transmissibility and impact of SARS-CoV-2 lineage B.1.1.7 in England
reference.author: CMMID COVID-19 Working Group, COVID-19 Genomics UK (COG-UK) Consortium

reference.unstructured: Tegally H , Wilkinson E , Giovanetti M , Iranzadeh A , Fonseca V , Msomi N , Mlisana K , von Gottberg A , Walaza S , Allam M , Ismail Mohale T , Glass AJ , Engelbrecht A , Van Zyl G , Preiser W , Petruccione F , Sigal A , Hardie D , Marais G , Hsiao NY , Korsman S , Davies MA , Tyers L , Mudau I , York D , Maslo C , Goedhals D , Abrahams S , Laguda-Akingba O , Alisoltani-Dehkordi A , Godzik A , Wibmer CK , Sewell BT , Lourenço J , Alcantara LCJ , Kosakovsky Pond SL , Weaver S , Martin D , Lesse lls RJ , Bhiman JN , Williamson C , de Oliveira T (2021) Emergence of a SARS-CoV-2 variant of concern with mutations in spike glycoprotein. Nature. Available: http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/33690265.
